message.content:
{
  "analysis": "The OCR extractions have been challenging due to the video quality and Zork's text-based interface. Let me take a final approach by carefully examining key frames and building the most accurate command list possible. I'll focus on getting clean, valid Zork commands.",
  "plan": "1. Create a final script that processes all frames with the best OCR settings. 2. Use the moves counter to track actual commands. 3. Clean up and normalize all commands. 4. Write the final solution.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "cat > /app/final_solution.py << 'EOF'
import os
import subprocess
import re

frames = sorted([f for f in os.listdir('/app/frames') if f.endswith('.png')])
print(f"Processing {len(frames)} frames...")

# Track moves and extract commands
prev_moves = -1
prev_cmd = ""
commands = []

for i, frame in enumerate(frames):
    frame_path = os.path.join('/app/frames', frame)
    result = subprocess.run(
        ['tesseract', frame_path, 'stdout', '--psm', '6'], 
        capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=10
    )
    text = result.stdout
    
    # Extract moves counter
    moves_match = re.search(r'Moves?[:\s]+(\d+)', text)
    moves = int(moves_match.group(1)) if moves_match else -1
    
    # Find the last line with > (most recent command)
    lines = text.split('\n')
    cmd = ""
    for line in reversed(lines):
        if '>' in line:
            parts = line.split('>', 1)
            if len(parts) > 1 and parts[1].strip():
                cmd = parts[1].strip().lower()
                break
    
    # Clean up command
    cmd = cmd.rstrip('._')
    cmd = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', cmd)
    
    # Only add when moves increase (new command entered)
    if moves > prev_moves and cmd and cmd != prev_cmd:
        # Filter out garbage
        if (len(cmd) >= 1 and 
            not any(c * 3 in cmd for c in 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz') and
            not re.match(r'^[a-z]$', cmd)):  # Skip single letters
            commands.append(cmd)
            prev_moves = moves
            prev_cmd = cmd

print(f"Extracted {len(commands)} commands.")

# Write to solution.txt
with open('/app/solution.txt', 'w') as f:
    for i, cmd in enumerate(commands, 1):
        f.write(cmd + '\n')
        print(f"{i:3d}. {cmd}")
EOF
",
      "duration": 0.1
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "python3 /app/final_solution.py 2>&1 | tee /app/final_solution_log.txt
",
      "duration": 120.0
    }
  ]
}
